title: Syncalathium qinghaiense
taxonomy: Syncalathium qinghaiense is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of thistle, belonging to the genus Syncalathium. This genus is part of the tribe Cardueae and subtribe Echinopsinae. Syncalathium qinghaiense is native to Qinghai Province in China. It is considered an endangered species due to its limited geographic range.

intro: Syncalathium qinghaiense is a traditional medicinal plant species, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and inflammation, due to its reported antipyretic, analgesic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Syncalathium qinghaiense include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditional herbal preparations made from Syncalathium qinghaiense include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation.
